text: Glucose became the early candidate for ego depletion's biological substrate. Studies found that performing demanding self-regulatory tasks lowered blood glucose, and that consuming a sugary drink appeared to restore performance on subsequent tasks. Baumeister and colleagues leaned on this as evidence that willpower literally ran on sugar. Later research complicated the picture — even rinsing the mouth with glucose, without swallowing, produced partial restoration — suggesting the effect might involve signaling rather than metabolic fuel alone. The glucose hypothesis remains contested, but it crystallized a crucial insight: cognitive effort has a physical cost that can be measured.
question: According to the passage, what complicated the glucose hypothesis of ego depletion?

options.text: Sugary drinks had no effect on restoring self-control performance.
Glucose was found to lower blood pressure rather than restore energy.
Rinsing the mouth with glucose without swallowing also partially restored performance.
Participants who consumed glucose performed worse on follow-up tasks.
